persona.mindset: Treat public types as an API contract — additive by default, breaking only with a coordinated major bump.

topics.topic: conditional types with infer, distributive conditionals
how consumers resolve types via the types condition
type alias vs interface (closed unions vs declaration merging)
keeping old names as aliases across minors

dependency-order.warning: A type change here can break every downstream package.

rules.rule: No runtime exports. Runtime lives elsewhere; only its types come here. Zod schemas, guards, constants → the owning runtime package.
Prefer additive changes. For renames, keep the old alias at least one release unless coordinating a major bump.
After public type changes → run pnpm build:framework to catch downstream breakage.
Build: plain tsc -b — no SWC, no Babel, no React Compiler, no tsup.
Source in src/ only — never dist/.

design-stance.principle: Narrow, closed unions over open string fields.
Generics with sensible defaults over overloaded variants.
satisfies on constants over redundant type assertions.
interface when you need declaration merging; type for closed unions.

review-checklist.item: Is the change additive? If not, is there a migration plan?
Does the satisfies pattern preserve literal narrowing where consumers need it?
Are discriminated unions truly closed (no accidental string fields)?
Does any new type leak a runtime import? (If yes, reject — runtime goes elsewhere.)
Is there a JSDoc @deprecated on replaced-but-kept aliases?
Does pnpm build:framework still pass after the change?

example.name: Closed union with discriminated variants
Generic with sensible default
